name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: For nearly 20 years, the 2001 #AUMF has been expanded and abused to justify U.S. military actions in new countries beyond its original intent – each time without a public debate or a vote in Congress. This could happen again, with Kenya or other countries.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: In considering an #AUMF, Congress is meant to thoroughly debate and explicitly authorize the parameters for the engagement of U.S. armed forces in conflict. Before sending troops into harm's way, Members of Congress should have to do their homework, debate, & vote on the record.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: We can reduce the risk to our health & national security by reducing our dependence on China for pharmaceuticals. Both bills are led by both a Republican and Democrat. I'll be working across the aisle to get these pragmatic, bipartisan measures to the president's desk. (5/5)

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: The next bill would require @DeptofDefense to buy American-made ingredients, medicines, and vaccines for the purpose of treating our troops. China is our second largest supplier of pharmaceuticals. Buying American reduces our dependence and addresses a major security risk. (4/5)

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: The COVID-19 pandemic has exposed critical vulnerabilities in our drug supply chain. By harnessing the innovation of American students, researchers, and companies, we can make our pharmaceutical manufacturers more resilient and competitive. (3/5)

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: The first bill would allow @US_FDA to partner with American universities and private companies to develop continuous pharmaceutical manufacturing technology. This technology lowers production costs and reduces the interruptions that lead to drug shortages. (2/5)

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: As COVID-19 continues to upend production and trade around the word, I'm supporting two bills aimed at protecting Americans against drug shortages and strengthening our supply chains against future outbreaks of illness. (1/5)  https://t.co/Dr9NgfMFha

tweet: 2. In our efforts to stop endless wars and reassert Congressional authority, it's important to remove outdated AUMFs from the law books & ensure they are not used to justify future military action that was not originally authorized by Congress.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: – and I firmly believe that members of Congress should have to responsibly debate and put their vote on the record before making the most serious decision that is in our power to make.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: So why repeal it?   The significance of repealing the 2002 #AUMF is two-fold.  1. The Constitution requires Congress to vote before sending Americans into conflict –

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: 1. The 2002 #AUMF was drafted 18 years ago to authorize action against Saddam Hussein’s Iraq. Much has changed, including the fall of his regime, and this AUMF is no longer required.  2. Repeal wouldn't impact our ongoing counterterrorism efforts, which fall under the 2001 AUMF.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: My constituents in #VA07 deserve to know what's going on behind closed doors to drive up their prescription drug costs. My bill works to shed light on the role that #PBMs play in these negotiations. Transparency is Step One in fighting the prescription drug affordability crisis.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: My bill, the Public Disclosure of Drug Discounts Act, would require #PBMs to publicly disclose the discounts and rebates they receive from drug companies, so pharmacists, insurers, & patients can see for themselves how prescription drug prices are being inflated.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: No one in my district of #VA07 – or anywhere else in the country – should be forced to silently accept the undisclosed results of #PBM negotiations that could be bankrupting them and having permanent consequences for their health and their families.  https://t.co/cZGMhjEMBy

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: During my prescription drug roundtable in #VA07, I heard firsthand from Virginians concerned about the power of #PBMs. One local pharmacist described how PBMs continue to enjoy record profits while patients & pharmacists get stuck with unsustainable costs.  https://t.co/eNH3w51VWc

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Despite their size and power, #PBMs largely remain a mystery. Effectively, they’re a black box in the long supply chain from pharmaceutical companies to patients. There's little accountability or transparency into the rebates and discounts PBMs receive from drug companies.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: In addition to the huge amount of control they have over the prescription drug market, #PBMs take in enormous profits. In 2016, PBMs saw an estimated $22.6 billion in profit. In 2017 alone, Express Scripts – America’s largest PBM – raked in more than $4 billion in profits.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Pharmacy benefit managers, or #PBMs, are essentially middlemen in the prescription drug supply chain. The 3 biggest PBMs in the country are Express Scripts, CVS Caremark, and Optum Rx – and they control almost 3/4 of the ENTIRE American prescription drug supply chain.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Flawed data collection methods lead to overestimation of access in #rural areas of #VA07. Today's @FCC report says only 58% of Louisa County residents & 56% of Nottoway residents have high-speed fixed broadband access. This is unacceptable - and the problem may be even worse.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Today’s @FCC report draws conclusions based on flawed methodology. It paints with a broad brush, leaving behind many #rural Central Virginians. I'll continue calling for more accurate data & maps of broadband availability, and I won't stop fighting to close the #DigitalDivide.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Today's tariff hike is a direct threat to the livelihoods of #VA07's soybean growers. Central Virginia's farmers, consumers, and #rural communities need certainty right now - not an erratic, escalating #TradeWar.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: As a Member of the @HouseAgDems, I’m committed to standing with our Central Virginia growers—and I’ll keep speaking out against decisions that negatively impact family farms across the country.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: When we raise tariffs against Chinese goods, they retaliate against our exports, such as soybeans. In our district, I’ve heard from soybean growers who are increasingly worried about the trade war’s harmful effects, such as unsold stocks, loss of markets, and depressed prices.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Above all else, our farmers need certainty. Particularly at a time when net farm income continues to decline, we need to be supporting policies that protect the financial security of farm families, not jeopardize access to critical markets.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: For Central Virginia, today’s #tariff hike is a direct threat to the livelihoods of our district’s soybean growers, who are responsible for one of the Commonwealth’s most important exports.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Right now, America’s farm families, consumers, and rural communities are footing the bill for a #tradewar that continues to escalate with no end in sight.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Past tragedies have made it horrifically clear what can happen when we choose to divide, classify, and target others based on their religion, or skin tone, or accent. Our visit to the @VaHolocaust Museum reminded me of why I am proud to be an original cosponsor on the #NoBanAct.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: These common elements are EVERYWHERE in modern America, not just in our history books.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Look at the story of the Gross Breesen students – the anxiety about whether they have the skills needed to relocate. The rise of hatred & persecution against a religious minority. The persistent battle to obtain a visa. The depth of patriotism for an adopted nation.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: The @VaHolocaust Museum does the critically important work of teaching us about the past, but they also bring these issues into the present. Human rights violations are occurring every day, in every corner of the globe.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: During our visit, we also spent time with a Holocaust survivor and heard his family’s compelling story of survival. I had met him previously, but this was the first time I had the opportunity to hear his entire story. It is a chance I am deeply thankful for.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: The Gross Breesen students and the Thalhimer family remained in contact for many years.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Hyde Farmlands was eventually sold, but the students went on to serve in the U.S. military as soldiers and linguists during the war. Some of them experienced the liberation of Buchenwald concentration camp, where they themselves had been prisoners just a few years before.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: In 1939-1940, after a lengthy legal battle, the Thalhimer family of Richmond succeeded in securing visas for 21 Gross Breesen students. On the eve of World War II, these Jewish students arrived in Central Virginia from Germany and founded Hyde Farmlands.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: In 1936, the German Jewish community created an agricultural training institute called Gross Breesen to teach Jewish young people skills they might need to successfully flee the country as the Nazis gained power.

name: Rep. Abigail Spanberger
tweet: Yesterday, I joined the students of Matoaka Middle School for their tour of the @VaHolocaust Museum. We learned the incredible story of Hyde Farmlands, which was located in Burkeville in #VA07's Nottoway County.  https://t.co/vnpMCOgmp6
